column3: Used to name representative groups who have loved Beppu's hot springs
column5: 別府の温泉は昔から、別府に住んでいる人や旅行客に愛されて来ました。
column6: Since long ago, Beppu's hot springs have been loved by people living in Beppu and by tourists.
column7: や gives examples in a non-exhaustive list, so these are representative groups rather than a complete list.

column3: Shows who has loved Beppu's hot springs by marking them as the passive agent
column5: 別府の温泉は昔から、別府に住んでいる人や旅行客に愛されて来ました。
column6: Since long ago, Beppu's hot springs have been loved by people living in Beppu and by tourists.
column7: The people who love the hot springs are marked with に in this passive expression.

column3: Indicates that being loved has continued from the past up to the present
column5: 別府の温泉は昔から、別府に住んでいる人や旅行客に愛されて来ました。
column6: Since long ago, Beppu's hot springs have been loved by people living in Beppu and by tourists.
column7: 〜てくる here shows a state that has continued up to now.

column3: Because it is located up on the mountainside, the town of Beppu can be seen from there
column5: 山の上の方にあるので、別府の町が見えます。
column6: Because it is up on the mountainside, you can see the town of Beppu.
column7: ので gives an explanatory reason and sounds a bit softer than から.

column3: You do the next action without changing the state of still wearing your clothes
column5: 鉄輪むし湯では、服を着たまま、薬の草、薬草の上に寝ます。
column6: At Kannawa Steam Bath, you lie down on top of medicinal herbs while still wearing your clothes.
column7: 例をもう一つ：靴をはいたまま入らないでください。

column3: The action applies only to the feet, not the whole body
column5: 足湯は、服を着たまま、足だけ温泉に入ります。
column6: In a footbath, you enter the hot spring with only your feet, while still wearing your clothes.
column7: だけ limits the action to the feet.

column3: Invites someone to try going to Beppu's hot springs and see how it is
column5: 別府の温泉に行ってみませんか。
column6: Would you like to try going to Beppu's hot springs?
column7: Here, 〜てみる appears as the polite invitation 〜てみませんか.
